question: Get the top 5 most frequently used module names from Python (`.py`) and R (`.r`) scripts, counting occurrences of modules in `import` and `from` statements for Python, and `library()` calls for R. The query should consider only Python and R files, group by module name, and return the top 5 modules ordered by frequency.
temp_SQL: SELECT module_name, COUNT(*) AS frequency FROM (SELECT REGEXP_SUBSTR("content", 'import\s+([\w\.]+)', 1, seq) AS module_name FROM SAMPLE_CONTENTS, LATERAL FLATTEN(input => SPLIT("content", '\n')) seq WHERE "content" ILIKE '%.py%' UNION ALL SELECT REGEXP_SUBSTR("content", 'library\s*\(\s*([\w\.]+)\s*\)', 1, seq) AS module_name FROM SAMPLE_CONTENTS, LATERAL FLATTEN(input => SPLIT("content", '\n')) seq WHERE "content" ILIKE '%.r%') GROUP BY module_name ORDER BY frequency DESC LIMIT 5;
final_SQL: SELECT module_name, COUNT(*) AS frequency FROM (SELECT REGEXP_SUBSTR("content", 'import\s+([\w\.]+)', 1, seq) AS module_name FROM SAMPLE_CONTENTS, LATERAL FLATTEN(input => SPLIT("content", '\n')) seq WHERE "content" ILIKE '%.py%' UNION ALL SELECT REGEXP_SUBSTR("content", 'library\s*\(\s*([\w\.]+)\s*\)', 1, seq) AS module_name FROM SAMPLE_CONTENTS, LATERAL FLATTEN(input => SPLIT("content", '\n')) seq WHERE "content" ILIKE '%.r%') GROUP BY module_name ORDER BY frequency DESC LIMIT 5;
